2604.06095.title: LLM4CodeRE: Generative AI for Code Decompilation Analysis and Reverse Engineering
2604.06095.paper_summary: Code decompilation analysis is a fundamental yet challenging task in malware reverse engineering, particularly due to the pervasive use of sophisticated obfuscation techniques. Although recent large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in translating low-level representations into high-level source code, most existing approaches rely on generic code pretraining and lack adaptation to malicious software. We propose LLM4CodeRE, a domain-adaptive LLM framework for bidirectional code reverse engineering that supports both assembly-to-source decompilation and source-to-assembly translation within a unified model. To enable effective task adaptation, we introduce two complementary fine-tuning strategies: (i) a Multi-Adapter approach for task-specific syntactic and semantic alignment, and (ii) a Seq2Seq Unified approach using task-conditioned prefixes to enforce end-to-end generation constraints. Experimental results demonstrate that LLM4CodeRE outperforms existing decompilation tools and general-purpose code models, achieving robust bidirectional generalization.
2604.06095.paper_summary_zh: 代碼反編譯分析是在惡意軟體逆向工程中一項基本但具有挑戰性的任務，特別是由於複雜混淆技術的廣泛使用。儘管最近的大型語言模型（LLMs）在將低階表示轉換為高階源代碼方面顯示出潛力，但大多數現有方法依賴於通用代碼預訓練，並且缺乏對惡意軟體的適應性。我們提出了LLM4CodeRE，這是一個領域自適應的LLM框架，用於雙向代碼逆向工程，支持在統一模型中進行從組合語言到源代碼的反編譯以及從源代碼到組合語言的翻譯。為了實現有效的任務適應，我們引入了兩種互補的微調策略：（i）用於任務特定語法和語義對齊的多適配器方法，以及（ii）使用任務條件前綴來強制執行端到端生成約束的Seq2Seq統一方法。實驗結果表明，LLM4CodeRE在性能上超越了現有的反編譯工具和通用代碼模型，實現了穩健的雙向泛化。
2604.06095.author: Hamed Jelodar et.al.
2604.06095.authors: Hamed Jelodar, Samita Bai, Tochukwu Emmanuel Nwankwo, Parisa Hamedi, Mohammad Meymani, Roozbeh Razavi-Far, Ali A. Ghorbani
